checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: No IntangibleAssets, Goodwill, or OtherAssetsNoncurrent line items were provided in the data set

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?
Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueNet=4910272437.55 (FY2010) vs SalesRevenueNet=5378550859.45 (FY2009) -- revenue declined ~8.7% while inventory rose ~25%
GrossProfit=2636138682.68 (FY2009) rising to GrossProfit=2457725606.10 on lower revenue (FY2010), i.e. gross margin expanded from ~49.0% to ~50.1% even as revenue fell and inventory ballooned

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: SalesRevenueNet quarterly: 797168033.89 (Q1'09) vs 1426132679.83 (Q3'09) vs implied ~1934M (Q4'09) show strong seasonal swings mirrored in earnings, indicating no artificial smoothing

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=77415105.48 (2008-12-27), 66944378.87 (2009-12-26), 58089167.08 (2010-12-25) -- allowance fell even as AccountsReceivableNetCurrent rose from 1353237364.98 (2008) to 1595635781.40 (2009)

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?
Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data spanning FY2007 through Q1 2011 provided across Assets, Revenue, COGS, GrossProfit, Receivables, Inventory, Cash Flow and Liability concepts

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature revenue recognition or extended/liberal credit terms (channel stuffing) during FY2009: receivables grew ~17.9% (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=1353237364.98 in 2008 to 1595635781.40 in 2009) while revenue fell ~15.7% (SalesRevenueNet=6378229609.75 in FY2008 to 5378550859.45 in FY2009), pushing implied days-sales-outstanding from ~77 days to ~108 days (DSRI ≈1.40), a level commonly associated with elevated revenue-quality risk.

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=1353237364.98 (2008-12-27) to 1595635781.40 (2009-12-26) while SalesRevenueNet fell from 6378229609.75 (FY2008) to 5378550859.45 (FY2009)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible release/under-accrual of warranty and bad-debt reserves to support reported net income: warranty accrual roughly halved from 159587308.87 (2009) to 91062098.54 (2010) and doubtful-account allowance fell from 77415105.48 (2008) to 58089167.08 (2010) even as receivables and business scale remained elevated, potentially reducing expense recognized in the period and inflating earnings.

Possible delayed write-down of slow-moving/obsolete inventory: inventory rose ~25% (InventoryNet=565773372.71 in 2009 to 707498746.44 in 2010) while revenue declined ~8.7% (SalesRevenueNet=5378550859.45 FY2009 to 4910272437.55 FY2010), yet reported gross margin simultaneously expanded (GrossProfit=2636138682.68 FY2009 to 2457725606.10 FY2010 on lower revenue), a combination consistent with insufficient inventory reserve build-up inflating both inventory asset value and gross profit.

rationale_evidence.quote: InventoryNet=565773372.71 (2009-12-26) rising to 707498746.44 (2010-12-25) while SalesRevenueNet declined from 5378550859.45 (FY2009) to 4910272437.55 (FY2010)

top_signals: Receivables grew ~18% while revenue fell ~16% in FY2009, pushing implied DSO from ~77 to ~108 days (AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=1353237364.98→1595635781.40; SalesRevenueNet=6378229609.75→5378550859.45)

Inventory rose ~25% in FY2010 despite an ~8.7% revenue decline, accompanied by simultaneous gross margin expansion (InventoryNet=565773372.71→707498746.44; SalesRevenueNet=5378550859.45→4910272437.55)

Warranty accrual and bad-debt allowance both shrank materially (ProductWarrantyAccrualClassifiedCurrent=159587308.87→91062098.54; AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=77415105.48→58089167.08) even as receivables/business activity remained large
Minor filing irregularities: a 10-Q/A restatement and multiple deregistration (15-12G/25-NSE) filings around 2009-2010
